text: I arrive as mobile founder checking the offer between meetings. My past: I am interested but impatient; if the site does not show proof fast, I will leave.
Today I want to: Quickly view a beta/demo and decide whether to request a free sample.
My life goal is: Spend my attention only on tools that can quickly improve product decisions.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: Betabots - Synthetic beta users with real product evidence

text: I see "Betabots - Synthetic beta users with real product evidence".
I think: I can see the page title, but not enough of the offer yet, so I am holding back.
My reaction: My first reaction is cautious interest; the promise sounds relevant, but I need proof fast.
Idea: show a real sample beta-user result immediately on arrival.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life is acceptable only if the next step quickly shows whether this can improve product decisions.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that the product could fit my goal, but I have low confidence until I see an actual demo or sample.
